canonical_question: What is the Kubernetes security checklist?
aliases: how to secure a Kubernetes cluster
Kubernetes RBAC network policy pod security

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: Pod Security Standards replace PodSecurityPolicy (removed in K8s 1.25, Oct 2022); Validating Admission Policy GA in K8s 1.30 (Apr 2024); User namespaces stable in K8s 1.34 (2025)

constraints: NEVER run workloads as root unless absolutely required -- enforce runAsNonRoot: true and drop ALL capabilities
NEVER leave etcd unencrypted or accessible without mTLS -- write access to etcd equals root on the entire cluster
NEVER use the system:masters group for regular authentication -- reserve for break-glass emergency access only
ALWAYS apply default-deny NetworkPolicies in every namespace before deploying workloads
NEVER store secrets in plaintext in manifests or environment variables -- use encryption at rest and external secret managers
ALWAYS run the latest supported Kubernetes minor version -- only the 3 most recent minors receive security patches

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Need Docker/container image security only, not cluster-level Kubernetes security
Need cloud provider-specific security (AWS EKS, GKE, AKS) rather than generic Kubernetes
Need application-level web security (XSS, CSRF, SQL injection)

inputs_needed.question: What is your Kubernetes deployment environment?
Which security area is your primary concern?
